Ships: 0800-1200: USFS Hartford, Chilean Ship Huascar, French Corvetter Herquelen, HBMS Constance

People: 1200-1600: A post mortem examination held on the body of William Baker (lands) showed that death ahd been caused by the effusion of serum in the brain. At 2pm called all hands bury the dead and read the burial service. Immediately after the body was sent ashore for burial, escorted by a funeral party under the Command of Lieut J.W. Carlin USN. Half masted colours until funeral party reached the shore, all the men of war in the harbor half masting with us
